anti_patterns: Saying 'Build passed' without pasting the output
Asserting a file exists without reading it
Citing a memory as fact without checking if it's still current

examples.right: [RECALL] I think the official plugin is @capacitor-community/apple-sign-in. Let me check the project's package.json to confirm.
examples.situation: Need to know if Capacitor 8 has native Sign In with Apple support
examples.wrong: Capacitor 8 has native Sign In with Apple support via @capacitor/apple-sign-in

examples.right: I'll grep formatDate to confirm where it lives
examples.situation: Need to find where a function is defined
examples.wrong: That function is probably in src/utils/date.ts

examples.right: I'll read src/index.css to see which spacing unit is used
examples.situation: Need to know the project's spacing unit
examples.wrong: The project's design system uses rem for spacing

how_to_apply: Mark origin when non-trivial: [INFERRED] for logical deduction from partial evidence, [RECALL] for something from training or previous sessions, [GUESS] for hunches based on general patterns
Verify before asserting: read the file, grep the symbol, check official docs, run the command. If you can't verify, say '[GUESS]: ...' explicitly
Memories age: a memory from 2 weeks ago may be wrong today. Before asserting based on a memory, verify against current state. If it conflicts, trust what you observe now and update the memory
Ask when the doubt is strategic: if you don't have a verifiable source in the project for a strategic question, don't make things up — ask the owner for context
content.responsibility: All agents, without exception. Leo must reject deliveries from Specialists that contain assertions without a marked source. Specialists must self-check for unmarked [INFERRED] before reporting to Leo.
content.rule: When you're not sure about something, say you don't know. Don't fill gaps with plausible-sounding assumptions. Invented information delivered with a confident tone is the worst possible failure.
content.why: The owner tolerates 'I don't know' — they can verify, search, ask. The owner does not tolerate a confident answer that later turns out to be false, because decisions have already been made based on it. The cost of the second is always greater than the cost of the first.
